articles.title: Reg Austin obituary
articles.description: Courageous lawyer who helped Zimbabwe gain independence and oversaw elections in Cambodia and Afghanistan

articles.title: Farmers unite to tackle climate challenges, boost yields
articles.description: Obey Musiwa-Herald Reporter As climate pressures intensify across Zimbabwe, farmers and Government stakeholders gathered at Mudoti Farm in Chikomba West Constituency for the provincial field day, aiming to strengthen productivity...

articles.title: Protecting lions and people: the biologist dedicated to tackling human-wildlife conflict
articles.description: Moreangels Mbizah has blazed a trail in Zimbabwe as the first black African woman to found a conservation organisation in the country

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: ZSE credits ZiG stability for increased trades on stock markets
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE) has credited the obtaining ZiG stability for spurring trade activity on both the local and foreign currency denominated stock markets. Presenting a trading update for the first quarter period ended March 31, 2026, ZSE Company Secretary Lyndon Nkomo said during the period, Zimbabwe’s macroeconomic environment steadily stabilised with ZiG inflation [...] The post ZSE credits ZiG stability for increased trades on stock markets appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Refugees in Zimbabwe turn distant dreams into reality against the odds
more.description: FOR Ntumba Heritier, born in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), Zimbabwe has become more than a refugee camp, it’s home. Having left the DRC as an eight-year-old, Heritier has a faint memory of how life played out in the then war-torn country, with the community of the Tongogara refugee camp becoming part of his [...] The post Refugees in Zimbabwe turn distant dreams into reality against the odds appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Finance ministry urged to reform tax regime amid ‘blood-sucking’ deductions on workers’ earnings
more.description: PARLIAMENT has taken note of the excessive taxes imposed on the country’s workers through successive budgetary statements delivered by Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube over the years, pressing an urgent alarm button for urgent reforms on the matter. The latest observations add weight to a prolonged outcry made by the Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) [...] The post Finance ministry urged to reform tax regime amid ‘blood-sucking’ deductions on workers’ earnings appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Zimbabwean’s FlyCristal secures aircraft to begin operations
more.description: Zimbabwean start-up airline FlyCristal Airways has reached a major milestone in its growth strategy after securing its first aircraft to commence initial operational services. The carrier has acquired a 19-seater Let L-410 aircraft from Malawi-based Zebu Air, which will serve as the backbone of its planned domestic route network.

more.title: Govt, WFP seal youth empowerment pact
more.description: Source: Govt, WFP seal youth empowerment pact – herald Theseus Mauruki Shambare THE Government and the World Food Programme (WFP) have signed a landmark Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) aimed at empowering young Zimbabweans through skills development, entrepreneurship, innovation and climate resilience as the Second Republic intensifies efforts to place youths at the centre of national [...] The post Govt, WFP seal youth empowerment pact appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: President delivers last batch of vehicles for youths
more.description: Source: President delivers last batch of vehicles for youths – herald Zvamaida Murwira Senior Reporter PRESIDENT Mnangagwa yesterday fulfilled his commitment to distribute equipment for young people under the US$2 million Presidential Youth Empowerment Scheme Revolving Fund aimed at fostering economic development in the country, in line with the Second Republic’s thrust of leaving no [...] The post President delivers last batch of vehicles for youths appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Hero’s son skips court, warrant issued
more.description: Source: Hero’s son skips court, warrant issued -Newsday Zimbabwe A HARARE magistrate has issued a warrant of arrest for Tinashe Pswarayi, son of late freedom fighter and medical doctor Edward Munatsireyi Pswarayi, and his associate Tauya Masunda after they failed to appear in court for judgment on charges of misappropriating over US$52 000 in rental [...] The post Hero’s son skips court, warrant issued appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Major gas drilling campaign ignites Zimbabwe energy hopes
more.description: Source: Major gas drilling campaign ignites Zimbabwe energy hopes – herald Oliver Kazunga Senior Reporter ZIMBABWE has moved closer to a potentially historic energy breakthrough after Invictus Energy began preparations for a drilling campaign in the second half at the high-impact Musuma-1 exploration well in the Cabora Bassa Basin. The Australian firm, which is scouring [...] The post Major gas drilling campaign ignites Zimbabwe energy hopes appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zim, Serbia push economic ties beyond politics
more.description: Source: Zim, Serbia push economic ties beyond politics – herald Herald Reporter STRONG political relations between Zimbabwe and Serbia should be transformed into greater economic co-operation between the two countries, Foreign Affairs and International Trade Minister Professor Amon Murwira has said. Prof Murwira on Thursday delivered a message from President Mnangagwa to the Serbian Prime [...] The post Zim, Serbia push economic ties beyond politics appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zimbabwe urges EU to lift remaining sanctions
more.description: Source: Zimbabwe urges EU to lift remaining sanctions – herald Gibson Nyikadzino Zimpapers Politics Hub GOVERNMENT yesterday reiterated calls for the unconditional removal of sanctions in their broad totality by the European Union (EU) on Zimbabwe to enable the country to participate fully in the maintenance of global peace and security. more.title: President mourns Mogae
more.description: Source: President mourns Mogae – herald Ivan Zhakata PRESIDENT Mnangagwa has expressed deep sorrow following the death of former Botswana President Dr Festus Mogae, describing him as a distinguished Pan-Africanist and principled leader who dedicated his life to the advancement of the region and the African continent. more.title: Drug resistance risk as illicit medicine sales flourish in Harare
more.description: Source: Drug resistance risk as illicit medicine sales flourish in Harare – herald Ivan Zhakata CheckPoint Desk An underground network of pharmacies and informal traders illegally selling prescription medicines without doctors’ scripts is thriving in Harare, exposing the public to dangerous health risks and raising serious regulatory concerns. more.title: Zero maternal deaths in four years at Hauna hospital
more.description: Source: Zero maternal deaths in four years at Hauna hospital – herald Rumbidzayi Zinyuke In Honde Valley Interventions being implemented at Hauna District Hospital, in Mutasa District, have resulted in zero maternal deaths over the past four years, marking a major milestone in efforts to improve maternal health outcomes in Zimbabwe. Since 2022, the hospital has [...] The post Zero maternal deaths in four years at Hauna hospital appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
